en.extract:
The Bornean frogmouth (Batrachostomus mixtus) is a bird species in the family Podargidae.  Some taxonomists consider it to be a subspecies of the short-tailed frogmouth, but others consider it to be a distinct species. It is found in Indonesia and Malaysia and is endemic to the island of Borneo. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical moist montane forest. It is threatened by habitat loss.
The species was discovered by Charles Hose on Mount Dulit in Sarawak, Borneo, and described by Richard Bowdler Sharpe in 1892 for the Bulletin of the British Ornithologists' Club.


References


External links
del Hoyo, J.; Collar, N.; Kirwan, G. M.; Garcia, E. F. J. (2017). "Bornean Frogmouth (Batrachostomus mixtus)". In del Hojo, J.; Elliott, A.; Sargatal, J.; Christie, D.A.; de Juana, E. (eds.). Handbook of the Birds of the World Alive. Barcelona: Lynx Edicions. Retrieved 3 September 2017.

The '''Bornean frogmouth''' ('''''Batrachostomus mixtus''''') is a [[bird]] species in the family [[Podargidae]].  Some{{who|date=September 2017}} taxonomists consider it to be a [[subspecies]] of the [[short-tailed frogmouth]], but others{{who|date=September 2017}} consider it to be a distinct species. It is found in [[Indonesia]] and [[Malaysia]] and is endemic to the island of [[Borneo]].<ref>{{cite book |title=Phillipps' Field Guide to the Birds of Borneo |author=Phillipps, Quentin |author2=  Phillipps, Karen |name-list-style= amp |year=2011 |publisher=John Beaufoy Publishing |location=Oxford, UK |isbn=978-1-906780-56-2 }}</ref> Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]] and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]. It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].

The species was discovered by [[Charles Hose]] on [[Mount Dulit]] in [[Sarawak]], [[Borneo]], and [[species description|described]] by [[Richard Bowdler Sharpe]] in 1892 for the ''[[Bulletin of the British Ornithologists' Club]]''.<ref name="Sharpe, 1892"/>
